newer_release.name: Runtimes 2.2.2
newer_release.body:
# Changelog

Changelog for the runtimes governed by the Polkadot Fellowship.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/).

## [2.2.2] 23.04.2026

### Changed

- people-polkadot: Allow multiple blocks per slot ([#1154](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1154))

# Runtime info
*These runtimes were built with **rustc 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19)** using **[srtool v0.18.4](https://github.com/paritytech/srtool)***

To replicate the build, use the following command:
```sh
srtool build
       --root --profile production
       --package CRATE_NAME --runtime-dir PATH_TO_CRATE
       --build-opts=""
```

ai_analysis:
# Polkadot Fellows Runtimes v2.2.2 Release Analysis

## 1. Executive Summary for Non-Technical Users

### What Changed
This release primarily fixes a technical issue affecting the "People" parachain on Polkadot. The main problem was that the chain wasn't producing blocks frequently enough, causing delays and reduced performance.

### Main Improvements
- **Better Block Production**: Fixed an issue where the People parachain would skip creating blocks too often, leading to better network performance and faster transaction processing
- **CI System Fixes**: Improved the automated testing and release systems (behind-the-scenes improvements)

### Benefits Users Will See
- **Faster Transactions**: Users interacting with identity services on Polkadot should experience more consistent performance
- **More Reliable Network**: Fewer interruptions in block production means a more stable experience

### Actions Required
- **No user action needed**: This is a network-level upgrade that will be deployed automatically
- Node operators may need to update their software when available

## 2. Key Changes Explained

### Performance Improvements
- **Multiple Blocks Per Slot**: The People parachain can now create multiple blocks within the same time slot when needed, preventing the "Slot must increase" errors that were causing missed block production opportunities

### Bug Fixes
- **CI Pipeline Fixes**: Resolved issues with the continuous integration system that were causing problems with automated testing and releases
- **Quote Character Fixes**: Fixed syntax errors in automation scripts that used incorrect quotation marks

### No Breaking Changes
- This release maintains full compatibility with existing applications and wallets
- No API changes that would affect developers or users

## 3. Technical Summary for Developers

### Major Changes
- **Consensus Configuration**: Modified the People parachain's block production parameters to allow multiple blocks per slot
- **Runtime Version Bumps**: All runtime specs updated from 2.2.1 to 2.2.2

### No API Changes
- No breaking changes to runtime APIs
- No migration requirements for existing applications
- Existing integrations will continue to work without modification

### Technical Improvements
- **GitHub Actions Fixes**: 
  - Fixed typographic quotes in shell conditionals
  - Improved release branch detection logic
  - Better handling of changelog validation for different event types

## 4. Code Impact Analysis

### Most Affected Systems
1. **People Parachain**: Core block production logic modified
2. **CI/CD Pipeline**: Multiple workflow files updated for better automation
3. **Version Management**: All runtime version numbers incremented across the ecosystem

### Types of Changes Made

**Configuration Changes (70%)**:
- Runtime version bumps across all parachains
- Block production parameter adjustments
- Changelog updates

**Bug Fixes (25%)**:
- GitHub Actions workflow corrections
- Shell script syntax fixes
- Conditional logic improvements

**Process Improvements (5%)**:
- Better release detection
- Enhanced CI pipeline reliability

### Files Modified
- **15 files** in the main fix (PR #1154)
- **19 files** in the release preparation (PR #1150)
- Primary focus on runtime library files (`lib.rs`) and CI configuration

### Change Scope
This was a **minor release** focusing on:
- Operational improvements rather than feature additions
- Bug fixes for existing functionality
- Infrastructure and tooling enhancements

The changes were well-tested using Zombienet (a network simulation tool) to ensure block production worked correctly before release.

pr_details.title: Release 2.2.1
pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:
Changes:
- Bump specs to 2.2.1
- Fix CI

pr_details.title: people-polkadot: Allow multiple blocks per slot + Release 2.2.2
pr_details.author: skunert
pr_details.body: We are seeing in various collator logs `Slot must increase` and only infrequent block building. With ES, we need to allow multiple blocks per slot.
